Amended Notice Of Motion Of Pacific Structures Inc. And Build Group, Inc. To Stay Action And To Compel Reference
Off Calendar Nov-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Pacific Structures, Inc. ("PSI") and Build Group, Inc.'s motion to stay action and compel reference is denied. Section 24.c.i. of the parties' Purchase Order states: "Consequently any disputes or claims between the Parties or any director, officer, partner, employee or agent of the Buyer, or any supplier or subcontractors relating to the Project shall be subject to one of the following procedures which shall be selected." (Cvitanovic, Ex. D (emphasis added).) The ADR processes that the contract contemplates are mediation, judicial reference, and arbitration. The evidence demonstrates that the parties selected mediation to resolve their dispute and have begun that process by selecting a mediator and exchanging documents per the mediation privilege. (Shea Decl., par. 9 and Ex. B [e-mails re mediation].) PSI's reply brief apparently concedes Doka's interpretation of the Purchase Order, i.e., the parties were merely obligated to choose one of the ADR schemes. It is now too late for PSI to back out of its prior election to mediate.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Oct-20-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-DEFENDANT BUILD GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Matter continued to November 10, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 302 pursuant to amended notice filed October 14, 2020.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
